question: A Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot app still sets `zeebe.client.cloud.port`. What does the properties-reference page say about migrating this property?

options.text: There is no direct replacement — the Zeebe client URL is now configured as an HTTP/HTTPS URL, so the port is part of the URL.
Rename it to `camunda.client.cloud.port`; the value carries over unchanged.
Rename it to `camunda.client.grpc-port`; gRPC has its own dedicated port property.
Set `camunda.client.zeebe.port` instead; the cloud prefix is dropped but `port` stays.

a.text: The migration table leaves the replacement cell empty for `zeebe.client.cloud.port` and explicitly hints: 'The Zeebe client URL is now configured as HTTP/HTTPS URL'. The port travels inside the URL.

b.text: `camunda.client.cloud.port` is not on the page; the cloud sub-namespace keeps `cluster-id` and `region` but not `port`.

c.text: There is no `camunda.client.grpc-port` property; the gRPC endpoint is given as a full URL via `camunda.client.grpc-address`.

d.text: `camunda.client.zeebe.port` does not exist in the 8.8 starter; ports are configured through the URL of `grpc-address` or `rest-address`.

explanation: On the deprecated `zeebe.client.cloud.*` table, `zeebe.client.cloud.port` has no replacement property. Instead, the Zeebe client endpoint is given as a full HTTP/HTTPS URL via `camunda.client.grpc-address` or `rest-address`, which already encodes the port.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
